title: Atomic write uses .tmp extension which can collide across concurrent writers
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
concurrency
## Location
src/session/mod.rs:33-38, 494-515
## Violation
atomic_write uses path.with_extension("tmp") as the staging file. Two concurrent persist_session calls (e.g. an end_session race with a timer-driven autosave) on the SAME session_id collide on /persist_dir/<session_id>.tmp — one writer overwrites the other's staging, the final rename is the loser of the race. Worse, list_sessions skips files whose stem is "latest" but does NOT skip .tmp files; a crash mid-write can leave a partial .tmp around that breaks file_stem checks elsewhere.

There is no file lock, no per-session mutex, and SessionManager holds the persist_dir as a PathBuf without a fs::File guard.
## Mandated refactor
Use a unique temp name (.tmp.<pid>.<uuid>) so concurrent writers never collide. After rename, sweep any leftover .tmp.* on startup. Or hoist persistence behind a dedicated Persister actor that serializes writes on a tokio mpsc channel.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
